ticketPurchase.title: Buy Tickets
ticketPurchase.roundClosedDesc: Please wait for the next round

howItWorks.label: HOW IT WORKS
howItWorks.title: How to Play
howItWorks.step2Desc: Buy tickets with 100 META to enter the draw.
howItWorks.step3Desc: A random seed accumulates with every ticket purchase. At draw time, hashes from 3 future blocks (+10) are combined with the seed via keccak256 to determine the winner — impossible to predict or manipulate.
howItWorks.step4Desc: Smart contract automatically sends prize to winner.

drawMechanism.title: On-chain Randomness
drawMechanism.subtitle: MetaLotto combines three independent entropy sources to select the winner. No single party can predict or manipulate the result.
drawMechanism.flow1: Every ticket purchase mixes the buyer's address, block number, and timestamp into the seed.
drawMechanism.flow2: When the round ends, drawBlock = current block + 10 is set.
drawMechanism.flow3: After drawBlock passes, anyone can call drawWinner().
drawMechanism.flow4: Block hashes of drawBlock, drawBlock-1, and drawBlock-2 are fetched.
drawMechanism.flow5: hash1 + hash2 + hash3 + seed + pool → keccak256 → winner index.
drawMechanism.seedDesc: Every ticket purchase mixes the buyer's address, block number, and timestamp with the previous seed. All purchases in the round accumulate, so even the last buyer cannot manipulate the outcome.
drawMechanism.seedNote: The seed starts at 0 at the beginning of each round and changes irreversibly with each purchase.
drawMechanism.poolDesc: The total META collected in this round (in wei), used as an additional entropy source. The final value is unknown until the last purchase is made.
drawMechanism.poolNote: Different pool sizes produce different results even with the same seed and block hashes.
drawMechanism.blockDesc: When the round closes, drawBlock (current block + 10) is set. Since that block hasn't been mined yet, its hash is unknown to anyone. Block hashes are also very difficult for miners to manipulate.
drawMechanism.blockNote: The draw must be executed within 256 blocks after drawBlock. If exceeded, the owner resets drawBlock.
drawMechanism.finalNote: By combining three independent entropy sources (3 block hashes, accumulated seed, prize pool), the draw guarantees fairness that no single participant can predict or manipulate.

history.title: History
history.subtitle: Check the results of past rounds
history.info1: All round results are permanently recorded on the blockchain.
history.info2: Winner addresses are masked to protect privacy.
history.info3: You can verify all transactions directly through the blockchain explorer.

manual.label: USER MANUAL
manual.title: User Manual
manual.subtitle: A guide to the on-chain lottery service on Metadium blockchain
manual.sec1Desc: MetaLotto is a fully transparent on-chain lottery (raffle) system running on the Metadium blockchain. All processes execute automatically via smart contracts, and draw results can be verified by anyone through blockhash. Even operators cannot manipulate the results.
manual.feat2Desc: 4 draws per day for fast results
manual.feat3Desc: 90% of the pool goes to winners
manual.sec2Desc: To use MetaLotto, you need to add the Metadium network to your MetaMask wallet.
manual.setup1Desc: Install the MetaMask extension from Chrome Web Store. For mobile, download the MetaMask app.
manual.setup2Desc: Add the network to MetaMask by entering the information below.
manual.setup3Desc: Switch the network to "Metadium Mainnet" in MetaMask. The MetaLotto site may also prompt automatic switching.
manual.sec3Desc: You need META tokens to participate in MetaLotto. You can buy META on the exchanges below.
manual.buyStepsTitle: Buying META and Transferring to Wallet
manual.buy1Desc: Sign up for one of the exchanges above and complete identity verification (KYC).
manual.buy2Desc: For Coinone, deposit KRW. For Gate.io/MEXC, deposit USDT.
manual.buy3Desc: Buy META at market or limit price on the exchange. 100 META is needed per ticket.
manual.buy4Desc: In the exchange withdrawal menu, select META, enter your MetaMask wallet address, and withdraw. Be sure to select the "Metadium" network.
manual.warningDesc: When withdrawing, always select the Metadium (mainnet) network. Sending to the ERC-20 (Ethereum) network may result in permanent token loss. We recommend a small test transfer first.
manual.how1Desc: Visit the MetaLotto website and click "Connect Wallet". MetaMask will open automatically — approve the connection.
manual.how2Desc: Choose how many tickets to buy (1-100). Each ticket costs 100 META. More tickets = higher chance of winning.
manual.how3Desc: Click "Buy Tickets" and a MetaMask transaction confirmation will appear. Check the gas fee and approve. Once confirmed, ticket numbers are automatically assigned.
manual.how4Desc: After the round ends, check draw results in the "History" tab. If you win, the prize is automatically sent to your wallet.
manual.sec5Desc: MetaLotto is NOT like traditional lotto where you pick numbers. It uses a raffle system — when you buy a ticket, a unique sequential number is auto-assigned, and the winning ticket is selected using a random number from blockhash.
manual.keyPrincipleDesc: Buy ticket → Auto-assign number (#001, #002...) → Generate random number from blockhash → winnerIndex = randomSeed % totalTickets → Ticket holder wins!
manual.exampleDesc: Below, white = my tickets (5), green = winning ticket.
manual.whyOneDesc: The modulo (%) operation always returns exactly one integer between 0 and (totalTickets-1). Since each ticket has a unique index, exactly one ticket matches. Unlike traditional lotto where multiple people can pick the same numbers, duplicate winners are structurally impossible.
manual.bh1Desc: Each ticket purchase adds the buyer's wallet address, block number, and timestamp to a cumulative seed value via hash function. This seed reflects all participants' actions and is unpredictable.
manual.bh2Desc: After the round ends, hash values from 3 future blocks (not yet created) are used. Since nobody knows these values until the blocks are mined, no one — including miners — can manipulate results.
manual.bh3Desc: The 3 blockhashes and accumulated seed are combined via XOR to produce the final random value. This process is transparently recorded on the smart contract and verifiable by anyone via blockchain explorer.
manual.fq1Q: How do I receive my winnings?
manual.fq1A: The smart contract automatically sends META to your wallet upon winning. No claim process needed. If the transfer fails, you can use the manual Withdraw function.
manual.fq2A: Yes, the more tickets you buy, the higher your chance of winning. 5 tickets out of 1,000 total gives you a 0.5% chance. Maximum 100 tickets per round.
manual.fq3A: Practically impossible. Future block hashes are used, so no one — including operators — can predict or manipulate results. All code is publicly deployed on-chain.
manual.fq4A: Metadium blockchain has very low gas fees. Gas for ticket purchase is less than 0.001 META — essentially negligible.
manual.fq5A: Yes, in extremely rare cases where the blockhash expires (256 blocks), making the draw impossible. In such cases, all participants receive a full refund.
manual.fq6Q: Can I use it on mobile?
manual.fq6A: Yes, MetaLotto is a responsive web app that works on mobile browsers. Using the MetaMask mobile app's built-in browser is the most convenient option.
manual.fq7Q: Where can I verify the contract?
manual.fq7A: Click the "Contract" link at the bottom of the MetaLotto site to view the smart contract code and all transactions on the Metadium explorer.
